methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $42M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Atomic AI is a Series A biotech company founded by Raphael Townshend that has raised $42M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector, including same- and adjacent-stage companies such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $42M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $84M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies with valuations ranging from $100M to $3.3B; the median across this set is $805M. The public-comp anchoring method is not applicable because the target's revenue is not disclosed, precluding use of the sector's 7.4x median P/S multiple. Public-comp analysis is therefore excluded from triangulation.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $71M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $444M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage timing and peer selection. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively to current capital deployed, while peer-median anchors to later-stage capital rounds. Key uncertainties include whether Atomic AI will achieve near-term clinical or commercial milestones that would accelerate toward the upper end, and how tightly the selected peer set reflects comparable risk and growth trajectory.
